preview.summary: Co-hosts Canada take on Qatar in a Group B clash at a buzzing BC Place. Jesse Marsch's most talented Canadian squad ever look to convert home advantage, while Qatar, qualifying on merit for the first time, lean on Asian Cup-winning experience to spring a surprise.
preview.homeForm: Canada arrive as co-hosts with what Marsch calls their strongest-ever squad, though captain Alphonso Davies is racing back from a hamstring injury.
preview.awayForm: Qatar qualified directly through the AFC third round with 14 points, their first qualification on merit after hosting in 2022.
preview.homeKey: Jonathan David, the talismanic striker leading the line for Les Rouges.
preview.awayKey: Akram Afif, two-time Asian Cup winner who makes Qatar tick.
storylines: Canada chase a statement win on home soil
Davies' fitness is a live question for the hosts
Afif and Almoez Ali test a Canadian back line under pressure
preview.h2h: Their only prior meeting was a friendly Canada won 2-0.
